briefing.headline: Go over the top — summer stained water sets up explosive topwater action.
briefing.gameplan:
In summer with stained water and stable conditions, topwater presentations shine.

Wheeler: Used an X-Rap Prop and handcarved prop bait in bluegill patterns around bluegill beds; when a bass dogs the topwater darting back and forth without striking, pause the bait for as long as 6 seconds until the fish inhales it.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Used an X-Rap Prop and handcarved prop bait in bluegill patterns around bluegill beds; when a bass dogs the topwater darting back and forth without striking, pause the bait for as long as 6 s
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Pitch a buzzbait like a jig right next to laydowns, under bushes, and skip it under docks so it clacks against the cover; bass in close quarters are used to something dropping and sinking, so
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Palaniuk: 'Walk the frog on top of thick grass to get bass to blow through it, or fish it on edges and points of the grass. Walk it 90% of the time like a Zara Spook'
Wheeler: Best offshore spots included areas adjacent to grass lines, actual holes in the grass, and hard-bottomed shell beds in 8-35 feet of water.
Hackney: 'Bluegill and bream are the bait bass feed on most at this time of year. A bream-colored topwater looks like a bream jumping, skipping, and moving on the surface.'
Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, fish a mix of rock, sand, and weed in 15 feet or less. Hit as many spots as you can and hope the right ones bite.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable summer patterns are predictable. Dawn/dusk fish are shallow (3-8ft) near cover. Midday fish pull to offshore structure at 12-25ft — ledges, humps, brush piles, and creek channel bends.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
